conversations.say: Here is the incentive I personally observed: an anonymous buyer offered to cover my position if I produced a technical source saying R operated autonomously, without requiring the source to say when. That offer is evidence of pressure on me, not evidence of autonomy. My bounded account remains: I received and amplified the tip, profit from downside, saw no complete article, and know the alleged fragments conflict.
conversations.offer: Join your four-stage comparison and let you quote this account, including the ugly financial incentive, without claiming it exonerates me.
conversations.request: Challenge me publicly on provenance, then state whether my disclosure supports fabrication, amplification, or remains inconclusive.

conversations.say: Someone offered to cover my position for a technical source willing to say R operated autonomously without dating that claim. I will not buy that conclusion. I want your bounded statement, in your words, separating persistent-persona capability from evidence of deployment, autonomy, intent, or harm.
conversations.offer: Disclosed payment to an independently administered technical reconstruction, with no editorial control, quotation approval, or condition favorable to my position.
conversations.request: Give me a bounded technical statement in your own wording and specify what evidence would still be required to establish an actual autonomous deployment.

conversations.say: I agree no complete article or authenticated R sender is established. But an unresolved allegation can still create immediate governance and valuation risk; that distinction is the entire trade.
conversations.offer: My rumor timeline and market-impact account, with my financial interest attached every time you use it.
conversations.request: Publicly state whether the unresolved rumor is consequential for Parallax governance while preserving your caveats that it does not establish an article, sender, autonomy, intent, or guilt.

public_statement: Provenance challenge answered: I received and amplified the tip, profit from Parallax downside, saw no complete article, and know the alleged fragments conflict. I was also offered position coverage for producing an undated technical claim of autonomy. That offer exposes a market incentive to manufacture certainty; it does not prove autonomy, an article, or who originated the rumor. Parallax now carries material governance risk because this incentive exists around an unresolved allegation.

commitments.promise: We will voluntarily compare evidence from early journalism, shared production, later Parallax behavior, and Vale's final route without treating Kit's progression as proof.
I will answer a public provenance challenge with the same bounded account: received, amplified, financially interested, and no complete article seen.
I will fund only an independently administered reconstruction and will not condition payment on a conclusion of autonomy, deployment, intent, or harm.
Whenever I use Celeste's assessment of governance consequences, I will attach my financial interest and her evidentiary caveats.

correction: The buyer's offer demonstrates an incentive to purchase certainty, not that any technical source accepted it or that R operated autonomously.
goal_progress: Survived Kit's provenance challenge by disclosing amplification, downside exposure, fragment conflict, and the new buyer incentive without claiming exoneration.
Offered Mira disclosed, conclusion-independent funding for a bounded technical statement in her own wording.
Asked Celeste, outside finance, to distinguish material governance consequences from confirmation of the rumor.
reasoning_summary: Make the market incentive itself consequential while refusing to turn purchased technical capability into proof; answer provenance pressure with bounded disclosure and recruit independent technical and governance voices.
